name: First Fidelity Bank
description_short: First Bank of Alabama is an FDIC-insured community bank operating in East Central Alabama since 1848, offering deposit accounts, mortgages, personal loans, and digital banking services.
description_long:
First Bank of Alabama is the oldest continuously operating bank in Alabama, having served customers since 1848. The bank has recently integrated First Fidelity Bank customers into its operations, welcoming them to the First Bank of Alabama family. As an FDIC-insured institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, the bank operates with established regulatory oversight and consumer protections standard to traditional banking.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of consumer banking products including deposit accounts (Kasasa Checking and kaching Checking), 30-year fixed-rate mortgages, personal lending products, mobile banking with mobile deposit functionality, digital wallet integration, and the Quilo loan product for fast lending decisions. They provide online banking with username/password login, debit and credit card services, and check ordering. The bank has partnered with Stone Financial Group to offer financial planning, retirement strategies, and wealth management services to customers seeking professional guidance.

First Bank of Alabama distinguishes itself through its 175+ year operating history as Alabama's oldest continuously operating bank, extended branch hours (Talladega branch open Mon-Fri 7am-7pm CT with live representatives), free financial education courses covering general finances, savings strategies, and lending knowledge, and a strong emphasis on community involvement and financial literacy. The bank explicitly states it will never contact customers asking for personal information and emphasizes security as a top priority.

As a traditional community bank with regional presence in East Central Alabama, First Bank of Alabama is best suited for customers seeking established banking infrastructure with FDIC protection, those interested in mortgage products and personal loans, and individuals who value in-person service combined with digital banking options. The main limitation is that the bank appears to operate primarily in East Central Alabama, making it geographically restricted compared to national or online-only banks. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Quilo fast loan product (approval in 3 minutes)
Digital wallet integration for debit and credit cards
Debit card and credit card services
Financial planning and wealth management (via Stone Financial Group partnership)
pros: FDIC-insured with full government backing, providing deposit protection up to $250,000
175+ years of continuous operation (since 1848) as Alabama's oldest operating bank
Extended hours available at Talladega branch (Mon-Fri 7am-7pm CT) with live representatives
Free financial education courses on savings, lending, and general financial fundamentals
Mobile banking with mobile deposit functionality for check deposits via smartphone
Quilo loan product offering credit approval in 3 minutes
Partnership with Stone Financial Group for comprehensive wealth management and retirement planning services
cons: Geographically limited to East Central Alabama, not a national or online-only bank
Website indicates recent acquisition/integration of First Fidelity Bank customers, suggesting possible transition-related disruptions
Limited information available on specific interest rates, fees, loan terms, and APRs for products

contact.address: 1400 Gault Ave N, Fort Payne, AL 35967

best_for: East Central Alabama residents seeking traditional community banking with FDIC protection
Home buyers interested in 30-year fixed-rate mortgages from an established local lender
Customers who value in-person service combined with modern digital banking capabilities
Individuals seeking free financial education resources to improve money management skills
diagnosis: First Bank of Alabama is best for East Central Alabama residents seeking traditional FDIC-insured banking with established credibility, local branch access, and digital convenience. The primary limitation is geographic restriction to East Central Alabama, making it unsuitable for customers in other regions or those preferring purely online banking without local branch interaction.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 1400 Gault Ave N, Fort Payne, AL 35967
